# v0.26.15

## release on 20260601
## description
## changes
Changes

fix: eliminate race in TestCacheFilter_ColdMissCoalescing (<a class="issue-link js-issue-link" data-error-text="Failed to load title" data-id="4523603213" data-permission-text="Title is private" data-url="https://github.com/zalando/skipper/issues/4034" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/zalando/skipper/pull/4034/hovercard" href="https://github.com/zalando/skipper/pull/4034">#4034</a>)

Problem

<code>TestCacheFilter_ColdMissCoalescing</code> was intermittently failing under  

<code>-race</code> with:

    --- FAIL: TestCacheFilter_ColdMissCoalescing (0.00s)
        filter_test.go:401: expected 1 upstream fetch, got 2

The test asserts that 50 concurrent cold-miss requests coalesce into  

exactly one upstream fetch via <code>singleflight.DoChan</code>.

The previous synchronization used a <code>sync.WaitGroup</code> (<code>wgIn</code>) where each  

goroutine called <code>wgIn.Done()</code> <strong>before</strong> calling <code>f.Request()</code>. The  

fetch stub blocked on <code>wgIn.Wait()</code>, intending to hold the singleflight  

open until all goroutines had joined it.

This had a  

<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Time-of-check_to-time-of-use" rel="nofollow">TOCTOU</a>  

gap: a goroutine could call <code>wgIn.Done()</code>, get preempted, and resume  

while the fetch stub had already unblocked from <code>wgIn.Wait()</code> but had  

not yet called <code>storage.Set()</code>. At that point the goroutine would call  

<code>storage.Get()</code> → <code>nil</code> and enter <code>DoChan()</code>. If the leader's <code>fetchFn</code>  

had returned in the interim, <code>DoChan()</code> would start a new singleflight  

round and fire a second upstream fetch. Under the race detector's  

overhead this window was wide enough to trigger reliably.

Fix

Replaced <code>wgIn</code> with a <code>missCounting</code> storage wrapper that embeds a  

<code>sync.WaitGroup</code>. Each goroutine calls <code>wg.Done()</code> inside  

<code>storage.Get()</code> at the exact moment it observes a cache miss - the last  

possible point before <code>coalesce()</code> → <code>DoChan()</code>. The fetch stub calls  

<code>wg.Wait()</code>, which durably blocks <code>fetchFn</code> until all N goroutines have  

confirmed a miss and are either inside <code>DoChan</code>'s waitlist or in the  

scheduler-opaque gap immediately before it. Only then does <code>fetchFn</code>  

call <code>storage.Set()</code> and return, closing the singleflight round.

The main goroutine's spin loop was replaced with a <code>fetchStarted</code>  

channel closed by the fetch stub on entry, eliminating all busy-waits.

Before

<a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/20266078/598245586-0fb1da49-0707-49b3-a49e-c83fc5e42299.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..cPUglkffacvYIEWfQwQBB0VSKgFd6Q8hdcxNZ-hN7tU"><img width="2000" height="1294" alt="Goroutine Missed
Window-2026-05-26-141411" src="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/20266078/598245586-0fb1da49-0707-49b3-a49e-c83fc5e42299.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..cPUglkffacvYIEWfQwQBB0VSKgFd6Q8hdcxNZ-hN7tU" content-type-secured-asset="image/png" style="max-width: 100%; height: auto; max-height: 1294px;"></a>

After

<a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/20266078/598245660-73b8ffe0-6563-4841-a18d-f2c46f46fe0f.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..nr3dwKAKPAN5nJt5egCjSV03UMF-v2iXczTAZu9ILQ0"><img width="2000" height="984" alt="Goroutine Missed
Window-2026-05-26-141426" src="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/20266078/598245660-73b8ffe0-6563-4841-a18d-f2c46f46fe0f.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..nr3dwKAKPAN5nJt5egCjSV03UMF-v2iXczTAZu9ILQ0" content-type-secured-asset="image/png" style="max-width: 100%; height: auto; max-height: 984px;"></a>

No production code was changed.

*** ** * ** ***

Multiarch Docker image

Multiarch Docker image is available in GitHub's docker registry:

    docker run -it ghcr.io/zalando/skipper:v0.26.15 skipper --help

Docker image

Docker image is available in Zalando's Open Source registry:

    docker run -it registry.opensource.zalan.do/teapot/skipper:v0.26.15 skipper --help
